cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The approach of relying on logic and inference from evidence rather than tradition or religious authority when explaining the natural world

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The systematic gathering of empirical data through the senses or instruments, which became foundational to Scientific Revolution inquiry and challenged Aristotelian deduction from first principles

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A systematic procedure developed during the 17th century involving hypothesis, controlled experiment, observation, and conclusion that replaced reliance on ancient authorities like Aristotle and Galen

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Ptolemaic model, endorsed by the medieval Catholic Church, that placed Earth at the unmoving center of the universe with all celestial bodies orbiting around it

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Copernican model placing the Sun at the center of the universe with Earth and other planets orbiting around it, which overturned Ptolemaic cosmology

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: German mathematician and astronomer whose three laws of planetary motion showed that planets travel in elliptical (not circular) orbits around the Sun, refining the heliocentric model

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Polish astronomer whose 1543 treatise On the Revolutions of the Celestial Spheres proposed the heliocentric model and launched the Scientific Revolution

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Italian astronomer who used a telescope to confirm heliocentrism by observing the moons of Jupiter and phases of Venus; tried by the Roman Inquisition in 1633 and forced to recant

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: English mathematician and physicist whose Principia Mathematica (1687) established the laws of motion and universal gravitation, providing a unified mathematical framework for the natural world

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: English physician who in 1628 described the circulation of blood pumped by the heart through a closed system of arteries and veins, overturning Galenic medicine

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Enlightenment-era faculty of disciplined thought and inference by which philosophes argued that individuals could discover truth, ethics, and just government without relying on clergy or monarchs

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Enlightenment-born field studying the theory and practice of government, power, and civic institutions through reason and empirical analysis rather than divine mandate

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A general term for the 18th-century French thinkers — Voltaire, Diderot, Rousseau, and others — who applied reason to questions of politics, religion, and society

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Lockean idea that all individuals are born with fundamental entitlements — most famously life, liberty, and property — that no government can legitimately take away

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Rousseau's concept that legitimate government rests on an agreement between the people and their rulers in which citizens consent to be governed in exchange for protection of their rights

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Rights considered so fundamental to human existence that they cannot be surrendered or transferred, a concept invoked by Jefferson in the Declaration of Independence

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The philosophical position, advanced by John Locke and Francis Bacon, that knowledge derives from sensory experience and experiment rather than innate ideas or tradition

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Thomas Hobbes's 1651 treatise arguing that in the state of nature life is 'solitary, poor, nasty, brutish, and short,' so people must surrender rights to an absolute sovereign for protection

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Thomas Paine's widely read 1776 pamphlet arguing in plain language for American independence from Britain and against monarchy, helping galvanize public support for revolution

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1776 document drafted largely by Thomas Jefferson that announced the thirteen colonies' separation from Britain and invoked Lockean natural rights and social-contract theory

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: English philosopher whose Leviathan argued that humans in a state of nature require an absolute sovereign to prevent constant war and disorder

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: English philosopher whose Two Treatises of Government argued that governments derive legitimacy from the consent of the governed and exist to protect life, liberty, and property

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: French Enlightenment writer famed for wit and biting satire who championed freedom of speech, religious tolerance, and separation of church and state

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: French political philosopher whose Spirit of the Laws proposed separating government into legislative, executive, and judicial branches to prevent tyranny

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Geneva-born philosophe whose Social Contract opened with 'Man is born free, and everywhere he is in chains,' arguing legitimate authority rests on the general will of the people

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: German philosopher who defined the Enlightenment as humanity's emergence from self-imposed immaturity, urging people to 'dare to know' (sapere aude)

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: French philosophe who edited the monumental Encyclopédie, a 28-volume compilation of knowledge intended to spread reason and secular thought across Europe

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: English-born political writer whose Common Sense (1776) and The Rights of Man popularized Enlightenment arguments for republicanism and independence on both sides of the Atlantic

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Second U.S. president and Declaration signatory who argued for a mixed constitution balancing branches of government, reflecting Montesquieu's influence

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Virginia planter and primary drafter of the Declaration of Independence who later served as third U.S. president and exemplified the application of Enlightenment ideals in politics

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Oppressive or arbitrary rule by a monarch or government that disregards the consent or rights of the governed — a central grievance of Enlightenment-era revolutionaries

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The American colonial rallying cry protesting Parliament's imposition of taxes like the Stamp Act on colonists who had no seats in the British legislature

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1787 framework establishing the United States federal government with separated powers and a Bill of Rights, reflecting Enlightenment principles of limited, representative government

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A form of government in which power rests with elected representatives and citizens rather than a monarch — the system established by the American and French revolutions

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The French representative assembly of the three orders — clergy, nobility, and commoners — convened by Louis XVI in May 1789 to solve a fiscal crisis, triggering the French Revolution when the Third Estate broke away

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The French political split between supporters of reform and constitutional change versus defenders of traditional monarchy and the old order, which widened into violent factionalism during the Revolution

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The July 14, 1789 storming of a Paris fortress-prison by a crowd seeking gunpowder, widely seen as the symbolic start of the French Revolution

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The August 1789 French document drafted by the National Assembly that proclaimed liberty, equality, property, and resistance to oppression as universal rights of man
cards.answer: Declaration of Rights of Man and Citizen

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The radical French Revolutionary political club led by Robespierre that dominated the National Convention and drove the Reign of Terror

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1793–1794 period of mass executions in France under Robespierre and the Committee of Public Safety, during which tens of thousands were guillotined as 'enemies of the Revolution'

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The beheading device adopted during the French Revolution as a supposedly humane and egalitarian method of execution, symbolizing the Reign of Terror

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The October 1789 march of thousands of Parisian women to Versailles demanding bread and forcing King Louis XVI to return to Paris, signaling the monarchy's loss of control

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Father Miguel Hidalgo's September 1810 call from the town of Dolores urging Mexican peasants to rise against Spanish colonial rule, launching the Mexican War of Independence

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The late-19th-century Filipino reform campaign led by ilustrados like Jose Rizal seeking representation and civil rights within the Spanish colonial system before turning to outright revolution

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 19th-century New Zealand conflicts between the indigenous Maori and British settlers over land ownership and sovereignty, resisting colonial expansion

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Communities of escaped African slaves in the Americas — especially Jamaica, Suriname, and Brazil — who established autonomous settlements and resisted re-enslavement, sometimes waging long guerrilla wars against colonial powers

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: British monarch whose taxation and coercive policies toward the thirteen American colonies provoked the American Revolution

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Commander of the Continental Army during the American Revolution and first president of the United States, who set precedents for republican civilian leadership

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: French monarch whose indecision during the fiscal crisis of 1789 and attempted flight to Varennes turned the Revolution against him; executed by guillotine in January 1793

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Jacobin leader of the Committee of Public Safety who directed the Reign of Terror in revolutionary France before being guillotined himself in July 1794

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: French general who seized power in 1799, crowned himself emperor in 1804, and spread Revolutionary legal reforms (the Napoleonic Code) across Europe through conquest

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Venezuelan-born military leader, known as 'El Libertador,' who led independence movements across northern South America and liberated Venezuela, Colombia, Ecuador, Peru, and Bolivia from Spain

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Catholic priest whose 1810 Grito de Dolores rallied indigenous and mestizo peasants against Spanish rule, beginning the Mexican War of Independence

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Prussian chancellor whose 'blood and iron' diplomacy and three wars (Danish, Austro-Prussian, Franco-Prussian) unified the German states under Prussian leadership by 1871

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Piedmontese prime minister whose diplomatic maneuvering and alliance with Napoleon III drove the northern Italian unification movement (Risorgimento) in the 1850s–60s

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Italian nationalist who led the Red Shirts in conquering Sicily and southern Italy (1860), then handed the territory to King Victor Emmanuel II to complete Italian unification

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Filipino priest executed in 1872 by Spanish colonial authorities alongside Gomez and Zamora (the Gomburza), whose martyrdom helped inspire the later Philippine independence movement

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Filipino ilustrado writer whose novels Noli Me Tangere and El Filibusterismo exposed Spanish colonial abuses; executed in 1896, he became the national hero of the Philippines

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Puerto Rican nationalist, abolitionist, and poet who authored 'La Borinqueña' and helped lead the 1868 Grito de Lares uprising against Spanish rule

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 18th- and 19th-century shift from handcrafted production in homes and workshops to mechanized, factory-based manufacturing powered by steam and later electricity

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 18th-century British transformation of farming through crop rotation, selective breeding, and new tools that boosted food output and freed rural laborers to work in factories

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The British process of consolidating open fields and common lands into privately owned, fenced parcels, displacing peasants into cities as wage laborers

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The rapid 19th-century growth of cities as rural migrants and immigrants flooded industrial centers seeking factory work, creating crowded tenements and new social problems

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The contrast between pre-industrial home-based manufacturing by rural families and the centralized, mechanized production in urban factories that replaced it

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The manufacture of large quantities of standardized goods using interchangeable parts, division of labor, and mechanization — pioneered in textiles, firearms, and eventually automobiles

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Unprocessed natural inputs — such as cotton, iron ore, and rubber — that industrial nations imported from colonies and peripheral regions to feed their factories

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The coal, iron, timber, and water resources that gave Britain (and later the U.S., Germany, and Japan) comparative advantages in launching industrialization

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Henry Clay's early-19th-century U.S. economic program of protective tariffs, a national bank, and federally funded internal improvements (roads and canals) to promote domestic industry

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1793 Rhode Island factory built by Samuel Slater that introduced British textile-spinning technology to the United States, launching American industrialization

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The railway completed under Sergei Witte in 1916 linking Moscow to Vladivostok, knitting the Russian Empire together and accelerating Russian industrialization

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1868 overthrow of the Tokugawa shogunate and reinstatement of imperial rule in Japan that launched rapid state-led industrialization and Western-style modernization

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: British-born mechanic who emigrated to the U.S. in 1789 carrying memorized textile-machine designs, founding the Slater Mill and transplanting the Industrial Revolution to America

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Russian finance minister under Nicholas II whose policies — tariffs, foreign loans, gold standard, and the Trans-Siberian Railroad — drove Russia's late-19th-century industrial surge

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The broad process by which non-Western states adopted Western technologies, institutions, and industrial practices in the 19th century to strengthen themselves against European imperialism

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1839–1876 Ottoman reform program that introduced secular law codes, modernized the military, built railways and telegraphs, and attempted to preserve the empire against European encroachment

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1860s–1890s Qing Chinese reform movement that adopted Western military technology and built arsenals and shipyards while leaving Confucian institutions intact, ultimately failing to halt imperial decline

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Large Japanese family-controlled industrial and financial conglomerates such as Mitsubishi, Mitsui, and Sumitomo that drove Meiji-era industrialization under close state guidance

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Early-19th-century Ottoman governor of Egypt who broke from Istanbul, modernized the Egyptian army along European lines, and promoted cotton-based industrialization

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Ottoman sultan who abolished the Janissary corps in 1826 and launched military and administrative modernization that set the stage for the Tanzimat reforms

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: U.S. naval officer whose 1853 black-ship expedition to Edo Bay forced Japan to end two centuries of seclusion and open to Western trade, precipitating the Meiji Restoration

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Japanese emperor whose 1868 restoration ended the Tokugawa shogunate and lent imperial authority to sweeping industrialization, constitutional reform, and military modernization

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: English carpenter and weaver who around 1764 invented the spinning jenny, allowing a single worker to spin multiple threads of yarn at once and launching the mechanized textile industry

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Scottish engineer whose 1769 separate-condenser steam engine dramatically improved fuel efficiency, powering factories, mines, locomotives, and ships throughout the Industrial Revolution

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: American inventor whose 1793 cotton gin mechanized the separation of cotton fiber from seed, transforming the U.S. South into a slave-based cotton-exporting economy

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: English scientist whose 1830s discovery of electromagnetic induction made the dynamo and electric generator possible, underpinning the Second Industrial Revolution

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: American inventor who in the 1840s developed the electric telegraph and the dot-dash code bearing his name, enabling near-instant long-distance communication

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: American inventor who in 1839 discovered vulcanization, the heat-and-sulfur process that made rubber stable for use in tires, belts, and industrial machinery

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: English engineer whose 1850s steel-making process blew air through molten pig iron to burn off impurities, making cheap mass-produced steel possible

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Scottish-born American inventor who patented the telephone in 1876, enabling the transmission of the human voice across electrical wires

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: American inventor whose Menlo Park laboratory produced the phonograph, practical incandescent light bulb, and the world's first electric power station, ushering in the age of electricity

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: American industrialist whose moving assembly line at the Highland Park plant (1913) cut Model T production times dramatically and made automobiles affordable for the middle class

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Italian inventor who in the 1890s sent the first successful wireless radio transmissions, making long-distance communication possible without cables

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Swedish chemist and industrialist who invented dynamite in 1867 and later endowed the prizes bearing his name, reshaping mining, construction, and warfare

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Hargreaves's 1764 hand-powered machine that spun multiple threads of yarn simultaneously, dramatically increasing textile output in cottage workshops

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The coal-burning power source, perfected by James Watt, that provided mobile mechanical energy to pump mines, run factory machinery, and drive locomotives and ships

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Eli Whitney's 1793 machine that mechanically separated cotton seeds from fiber, fueling U.S. cotton exports and the expansion of plantation slavery

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Standardized components manufactured to precise specifications so that any one will fit any assembly, a manufacturing principle pioneered for U.S. firearms and crucial to mass production

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The heat-and-sulfur process, discovered by Charles Goodyear, that made rubber elastic and durable across temperatures, enabling tires, hoses, and industrial machinery

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1850s steel-making method that blew air through molten pig iron to burn off impurities, enabling the mass production of cheap, strong steel for rails, ships, and skyscrapers

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The form of energy — harnessed through dynamos, generators, and power stations in the late 19th century — that powered lighting, streetcars, motors, and the Second Industrial Revolution

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The physical phenomenon, explored by Michael Faraday, describing the relationship between electric currents and magnetic fields — the basis of generators, motors, and telegraphy

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Henry Ford's moving production technique in which workers stay in place while the product moves past on a conveyor belt, each adding a single task — a defining innovation of mass production

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The engine that burns fuel inside a cylinder to drive a piston, developed in the late 19th century and the mechanical heart of automobiles, motorcycles, and early aircraft

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The wireless electromagnetic communication technology pioneered by Marconi that allowed long-distance messages without cables, revolutionizing maritime and military signaling

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Alexander Graham Bell's 1876 invention that transmitted the human voice across electrical wires, enabling real-time long-distance conversation

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Alfred Nobel's 1867 nitroglycerin-based explosive, safer and more stable than earlier compounds, widely used in mining, tunneling, and military applications

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Scottish philosopher whose 1776 Wealth of Nations argued that free markets, the 'invisible hand,' and the division of labor generate prosperity, laying the foundation of modern capitalism

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: British mining magnate and imperialist who made a fortune through De Beers diamond monopoly in southern Africa and championed expansive British colonization of the continent

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A company that operates in multiple countries — with factories, subsidiaries, or markets across borders — reshaping late-19th-century global trade and labor

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: An economic system in which private individuals and firms own the means of production and pursue profit in competitive markets, with prices set by supply and demand

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The organized public market where shares of corporations are bought and sold, enabling firms to raise capital and investors to speculate on ownership stakes

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The control of an entire industry or market by a single company, allowing it to set prices and eliminate competitors — a hallmark of late-19th-century trusts like Standard Oil

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The cultural emphasis on acquiring and displaying mass-produced goods, which emerged as industrial output and disposable incomes grew in the late 19th century

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: John D. Rockefeller's late-19th-century American oil company that, at its peak, refined about 90% of U.S. petroleum, becoming the textbook example of monopoly broken up by the Sherman Antitrust Act

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The American fruit corporation that dominated banana production in Central America, backed by U.S. diplomatic and military interventions, creating the 'banana republic' phenomenon

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The diamond-mining and marketing company founded by Cecil Rhodes in 1888 that monopolized the global diamond trade from southern Africa

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Hong Kong and Shanghai Banking Corporation, founded in 1865 to finance trade between Britain, China, and India, becoming one of the first great multinational banks

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Anglo-Dutch consumer-goods multinational formed in 1929 from the merger of Lever Brothers and Margarine Unie, exemplifying the global reach of late-industrial corporations

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Workers' organizations formed in the 19th century to bargain collectively for higher wages, shorter hours, and safer conditions, often facing violent opposition from owners and governments

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The urban middle class of property-owners, merchants, and professionals that Marx identified as the capitalist class owning the means of production and exploiting wage labor

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: In Marxist theory, the industrial working class that earns wages by selling its labor and, according to Marx, must eventually overthrow capitalism in revolution

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1848 pamphlet by Marx and Engels declaring that 'the history of all hitherto existing society is the history of class struggles' and calling on workers of the world to unite

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: In Marxist analysis, the factories, machinery, land, and capital used to produce goods — ownership of which defines the bourgeoisie and which Marx argued should belong to the proletariat

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The philosophy, advanced by Jeremy Bentham and John Stuart Mill, that morality and policy should be judged by whether they produce 'the greatest good for the greatest number'

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The right to vote in political elections, gradually extended during the 19th and early 20th centuries to non-property-owning men, then to women, through reform movements and amendments

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: German philosopher and economist whose Communist Manifesto and Das Kapital argued that class conflict drives history and that capitalism would inevitably give way to socialist revolution

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: British parliamentarian whose 1832 report on conditions in textile factories documented horrific child labor and led to the Factory Act of 1833 restricting the employment of children

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: English philosopher and utilitarian whose On Liberty defended free speech and individual autonomy, and whose Subjection of Women argued for gender equality

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Late-Qing dowager empress who dominated Chinese politics from behind the throne for decades and backed conservative resistance to reform, contributing to the dynasty's collapse in 1911

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A cheap, overcrowded, poorly ventilated urban apartment building that housed industrial workers and immigrants in 19th-century cities like New York and London

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A densely populated, impoverished urban district marked by substandard housing, poor sanitation, and widespread disease — a hallmark of 19th-century industrial cities

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The industrial-era social class of factory laborers who earned hourly wages in mills, mines, and workshops and occupied the bottom of the new urban economic hierarchy

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The widespread 19th-century industrial practice of employing children as young as five or six in dangerous factories and mines, which prompted reform laws like Britain's 1833 Factory Act

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 19th-century middle-class ideology that confined women to the home as caretakers of moral and domestic life while men pursued careers in the public sphere

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1848 New York gathering organized by Elizabeth Cady Stanton and Lucretia Mott that produced the Declaration of Sentiments and launched the U.S. women's suffrage movement

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Olympe de Gouges's 1791 counterpart to the French Declaration of the Rights of Man, arguing that women were born with identical natural rights and should enjoy full civic equality
cards.answer: Declaration of Rights of Women and the Female Citizen

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 18th- and 19th-century reform movement to end slavery and the slave trade, driven by Enlightenment ideas and Christian moral arguments and championed by figures like Wilberforce and Douglass

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: American reformer who organized the 1848 Seneca Falls Convention and co-authored the Declaration of Sentiments, becoming a foundational voice of the U.S. women's rights movement

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: French playwright who authored the 1791 Declaration of the Rights of Women and the Female Citizen, then was guillotined during the Reign of Terror for her political writings

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: English writer whose 1792 Vindication of the Rights of Woman argued that women's apparent inferiority resulted from lack of education, not nature, helping inspire later feminism

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Danish-American journalist whose 1890 book How the Other Half Lives used photography to expose tenement life in New York City, galvanizing urban Progressive reform

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: English physician who in 1796 developed the first vaccine, using cowpox to protect against smallpox — a founding breakthrough of modern immunology

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: French chemist whose germ theory of disease, pasteurization process, and vaccines for rabies and anthrax transformed medicine and public health in the late 19th century

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: British evangelical politician whose decades of parliamentary campaigning secured the 1807 abolition of the slave trade and, just before his death in 1833, abolition of slavery in the British Empire

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Formerly enslaved American abolitionist whose autobiographies and oratory — and newspaper The North Star — made him one of the most powerful voices against U.S. slavery
